blocks:
Sūrat Al-Bayyinah

Surah Lam Yakun. It is also named Surah Al-Bariyyah and Surah Al-Qiyamah. It is مدنية (Medinan), and it is said مكيّة (Meccan). It consists of nine verses (آيات). The Basran count (بصري) is eight. In the remaining counts, the point of difference is the verse مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ (sincere to Him in religion), which is counted by the Basran school (بصريّ). بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ. (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) لَمْ يَكُنِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ مُنْفَكِّينَ حَتَّى تَأْتِيَهُمُ الْبَيِّنَةُ ** (1) The faithless among the People of the Book and the polytheists would not desist [from their disbelief] until the manifest proof came to them. Al-Qummi said: He means Quraysh. He said: They remained in their disbelief until the Clear Proof (البيّنة) came to them. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, الْبَيِّنَةُ ** (the Clear Proof) is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And in al-Majma': The wording (اللّفظ) is the wording of the future tense (الاستقبال), but its meaning (معناه) is the past tense (المضيّ).

(٢) رَسُولٌ مِنَ اللهِ ** (2) a messenger from Allah, (This is) an explanation for الْبَيِّنَةُ ** (the clear proof). ** يَتْلُوا صُحُفاً مُطَهَّرَةً ** (who recites purified scrolls) (These are) in the heaven; none touches them except the purified angels. (٣) فِيها كُتُبٌ قَيِّمَةٌ ** (3) in which are upright writings. (These are) writings that are straight, just, not possessing crookedness (عِوَج). And it was said: purified from falsehood (الباطل). And what is meant by the scrolls (الصحف) is what the scrolls contain of what is written in them, because the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to recite from memory, not from a book. But when He recited the like of what is in the scrolls, He was like the one reciting them.

** وَما تَفَرَّقَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ ٤ ** (And those who were given the Book did not divide) regarding what they were upon ** إِلَّا مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَتْهُمُ الْبَيِّنَةُ ** (except after the manifest proof had come to them.) It was said, meaning they had remained united in affirming Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم until Allah sent Him. But when He was sent, they divided regarding His matter and disagreed, so some of them believed in Him and others disbelieved. Al-Qummi said: When the Messenger of Allah came to them with the Quran, they opposed Him and divided after Him.

(٥) وَما أُمِرُوا إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُوا اللهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ ** (5) though they were not commanded except to worship Allah, dedicating the religion to Him as ḥunafā' (monotheists), meaning, they do not associate partners with Him. حُنَفاءَ ** inclining away from the deviant beliefs (العقائد الزايغة). Al-Qummi said: [meaning] manifest (ظاهرين). وَيُقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ وَيُؤْتُوا الزَّكاةَ وَذلِكَ دِينُ الْقَيِّمَةِ ** and maintain the prayer and give the zakat. That is the upright religion. meaning, the religion (دين) of the upright community (الملّة القيّمة).

(٦) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ وَالْمُشْرِكِينَ فِي نارِ جَهَنَّمَ خالِدِينَ فِيها

(6) Indeed, the faithless among the People of the Book and the polytheists shall be in the Fire of hell, to remain in it forever. Al-Qummi said: Allah sent down the Quran upon them, but they apostatized, disbelieved, and disobeyed the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). أُولئِكَ هُمْ شَرُّ الْبَرِيَّةِ ** They are the worst of creatures. meaning the creation (الخليقة). And al-Bari'ah (البرئية) was read with a hamzah (بالهمزة).

(٧)** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ أُولئِكَ هُمْ خَيْرُ الْبَرِيَّةِ ** (7) Indeed, those who have faith and do righteous deeds—it is they who are the best of creatures. Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the Household of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them).

In al-Amali, narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah, he said: "We were with the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم when Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام approached. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: 'Your brother has come to you.' Then He turned towards the Ka'bah and struck it with His hand, then He said: 'By Him in Whose hand is my soul, verily this man and his followers (شيعته) are the successful ones (الفائزون) on the Day of Resurrection.' Then He said: 'He is the first among you in faith with me, the most fulfilling of Allah's covenant, the most steadfast among you in Allah's command, the most just among you towards the subjects (الرعيّة), the most equitable among you in distribution (بالسويّة), and the greatest among you in merit (مزيّة) before Allah.' He said: Then ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ أُولئِكَ هُمْ خَيْرُ الْبَرِيَّةِ was revealed. He said: The Companions of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, when Ali approached, would say: 'The best of creatures (خَيْرُ الْبَرِيَّةِ) has come.'"

And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding this Ayah: He turned to Ali عليه السلام and said: "By Allah, they are you and your followers (شيعتك), O Ali. Your appointed time and their appointed time is the Pond (الحوض) tomorrow, bright-faced (غرّ), with white blazes (محجّلين), crowned (متوّجين)."

In al-Majma': That which is similar in meaning. And in al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, he said: "They are our followers (شيعتنا), the People of the House (أهل البيت)."

(8) جَزاؤُهُمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّاتُ عَدْنٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ خالِدِينَ فِيها أَبَداً رَضِيَ اللهُ عَنْهُمْ وَرَضُوا عَنْهُ ** (8) Their reward is with their Lord: gardens of Eden, with streams running in them, to abide in them forever. Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. because He has granted them the utmost of their desires. ذلِكَ لِمَنْ خَشِيَ رَبَّهُ (That is for him who fears his Lord), for fear (الخشية) is the pivot (ملاك الأمر) of the matter and the motivator for every good.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said to a man from the Shi'a: "You are the people of contentment (الرضا) with Allah, exalted is His mention. He is pleased with you, and the angels are your brethren in goodness. So, when you strive (اجتهدتم), supplicate (ادعوا), and when you become heedless (غفلتم), exert yourselves (اجهدوا). And you are **خَيْرُ الْبَرِيَّةِ ** (the best of creation). Your homes are a Paradise for you, and your graves are a Paradise for you. For Paradise you were created, and in Paradise is your bliss, and to Paradise you shall return."

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "Whoever recites Surah Lam Yakun (Surah al-Bayyinah) will be absolved of polytheism (الشرك) and admitted into the religion of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will resurrect him as a believer and reckon with him an easy reckoning (حساباً يسيراً)."
